race.name: Mt. Washington Auto Road Bicycle Hillclimb
race.display_name: Mt. Washington Auto Road Bicycle Hillclimb (MWARBH)
race.tagline: 7.6 miles. 4,678 feet. Average grade 12%. The highest peak in the Northeast, home to the worst weather in America. The toughest hill climb in the United States since 1973.

vitals.registration: Online via BikeReg. Opens March 1. Sells out. $350 entry. Practice ride registration opens April 1.
vitals.feed_zones: Summit support: water, portable toilets, first aid, commemorative fleece blankets, finisher medals.
vitals.cutoff_time: One-way uphill — riders finish at the summit. Support drivers descend from summit around noon.
route_options: Full Hillclimb: 7.6 miles, 4,678 ft gain — Auto Road base to Mt. Washington summit (6,288 ft)

climate.primary: White Mountains summer — extreme summit weather on the mountain with the 'worst weather in America'
climate.description: Mid-August at the Auto Road base starts mild (60-75F) but conditions deteriorate rapidly with altitude. Mt. Washington holds the record for the highest wind gust directly measured at the Earth's surface: 231 mph. The summit's yearly average temperature is 33.9F — no temperature above 72F has ever been recorded there. Even on a calm August race day, summit conditions can include fog, rain, high winds, and temperatures 20-30 degrees colder than the base. The weather observatory on the summit exists specifically because this mountain produces some of the most extreme weather on Earth.
challenges: Mt. Washington holds the world record for highest observed wind gust (231 mph) — summit winds of 40-60 mph are routine
Temperature drop of 20-30F from base to summit — hypothermia risk is real even in August
Fog and low visibility common above treeline — summit can be socked in with zero visibility
Rain, sleet, and even snow possible at the summit in any month of the year
Extreme wind chill above treeline with no shelter for the final exposed miles

terrain.primary: Paved mountain auto road — extreme sustained gradient
terrain.surface: Fully paved as of 2022. The Mt. Washington Auto Road was completely paved from base to summit in June 2022, ending decades of mixed pavement and gravel. Prior to 2022, a gravel section around mile 5 added difficulty. The road is well-maintained but narrow — a single-lane mountain auto road with no guardrails in many sections.
features: 7.6 miles of continuous climbing from 1,600 ft to 6,288 ft
Average gradient 12% — one of the steepest sustained road grades raced anywhere in the US
Extended sections of 18% gradient through the middle miles
Final 50 yards at 22% — the steepest ramp to the finish
61% of the route (4.5 miles) between 10-15% grade
11% of the route (0.8 miles) between 15-20% grade
Steepest quarter-mile averages 16%, steepest mile averages 13.8%
Treeline crossing at approximately 4,400 ft — landscape transforms to exposed alpine rock
Single switchback near mile 2.9 — the only directional change on the course

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 71. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 69.

logistics.lodging_strategy: Gorham NH (8 miles north) is the primary lodging hub: Top Notch Inn, Town and Country Inn and Resort, Northern Peak Motor Inn, and various motels from $58-150/night. The Glen House (closest to the Auto Road base) is a premium option. Jackson NH (12 miles south) offers charming inns and B&Bs. North Conway (25 miles south) has the most lodging volume. Book early — race weekend fills quickly in this small-town region. Alpine Garden Glamping is a unique option near the base.

biased_opinion.summary:
There is no subtlety to the Mt. Washington Auto Road Bicycle Hillclimb. It is 7.6 miles of going uphill at 12% average gradient until you reach the summit of the highest peak in the Northeastern United States, a mountain so hostile that it holds the world record for the highest wind gust ever directly measured on Earth's surface. Since 1973, cyclists have lined up at the base of the Auto Road to race the clock, the gradient, and whatever weather the mountain decides to deliver. Tom Danielson did it in 49:24. Tyler Hamilton did it four times (though his records were vacated). Ian Boswell set the current mark at 50:18. Most riders take 60-90 minutes. All of them suffer.

The Mt. Washington Hillclimb is not a long race. It is not a technical race. It is not an altitude race. It is a gradient race on a mountain with a personality disorder — a 6,288-foot peak in New Hampshire that produces weather systems normally reserved for peaks three times its height. The 2022 completion of the road paving project reset the records and the conversation: every time set on the fully paved road is a modern mark. The 50th edition that year was a line in the sand. Everything before it was a different race on a different surface. Everything after is the pure, paved truth: how fast can you ride uphill at 12% for 7.6 miles into the worst weather in America?

This is not a race you enter casually. The $350 entry fee, the remote New Hampshire location, and the single-digit mileage all conspire to make this a deliberate choice. You come to Mt. Washington because you want to test yourself against the steepest sustained road climb in the Eastern United States. You stay because the summit finish — fleece blanket in hand, legs destroyed, staring out across the Presidential Range — is one of the most earned moments in American cycling.
strengths: The steepest sustained road climb race in the Eastern US: 12% average, 18% extended sections, 22% finish
50+ years of continuous history since 1973 — one of the oldest cycling events in America
Summit finish at 6,288 ft on the highest peak in the Northeast with panoramic views into four states
Mt. Washington's extreme weather adds genuine unpredictability and danger to every edition
Winners list includes former WorldTour professionals — legitimate competitive pedigree
weaknesses: 7.6 miles is extremely short — most riders finish in under 90 minutes
Remote New Hampshire location: nearest major airport is 2+ hours away
$350 for a sub-hour race is expensive on a per-minute basis
One-way uphill only — if you want varied terrain or descending, this is not your event
biased_opinion.bottom_line: If you want to know what 12% average gradient feels like for 50 straight minutes, the Mt. Washington Hillclimb has been waiting since 1973. Bring the fleece blanket.

final_verdict.one_liner: 7.6 miles. 4,678 feet. 12% average grade. Summit finish on the mountain with the worst weather in America. Since 1973.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if you are a hill climb specialist, power-to-weight obsessive, or simply need to know if you can ride up the steepest sustained paved climb in the Eastern US. Yes if you want a bucket-list summit finish on the highest peak in the Northeast. Skip if you want distance, technical terrain, or a mass-participation festival atmosphere. Skip if remote New Hampshire logistics are a dealbreaker.
final_verdict.alternatives: If you want a higher-altitude US hill climb with more miles: try Mt. Evans Hill Climb (27 miles to 14,130 ft). If you want a European mountain time trial with global prestige: try Mont Ventoux Challenge. If you want East Coast gravel with serious climbing: try Rasputitsa.
